pattern: A contiguous run has no inversions exactly when it never dips from one element to the next, so once every position knows how far its own non-decreasing run reaches backward, a query's answer becomes a triangular count plus a running total, both readable in constant time.
hints: A subarray has zero inversions if and only if it never decreases from one adjacent element to the next -- so 'stable' is really just 'non-decreasing', which you can detect locally without comparing every pair.

For each position, precompute the start of the longest non-decreasing run ending there; for a query starting at l, every position sharing that same run as l contributes subarrays entirely inside [l, that run's end], which is a simple triangular-number count.

Past the end of l's own run, every later position's natural run start already sits at or after l, so its unclamped run length can just be added straight from a precomputed running total -- no need to reprocess the array per query.

edge_inputs.description: single-element query on a longer array
entire array is one non-decreasing run, whole-array query
the query window starts mid-run, requiring the run to be clamped from the left
strictly decreasing array, only single-element subarrays are stable
multiple queries over the same array with overlapping ranges

stress.description: random array with random query ranges, sized toward the constraint maximum
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    q = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    queries = []
    for _ in range(q):
        l = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        r = rng.randint(l, n - 1)
        queries.append([l, r])
    return [nums, queries]
